name: Great Chocolate Goo Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter over low heat and allow it to cool to room temperature.
2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
3. Cut a round of waxed paper to fit the bottom of a 9 inch springform pan.
4. Butter the sides of the pan and one side of the waxed paper. Place the paper in the pan, butter side up.
5. In a saucepan over very low heat, heat the chocolate and milk, stirring constantly, just until the chocolate is melted.
6. Pour the mixture into the large bowl of an electric mixer.
7. Beat on low speed, just to smooth out any lumps of chocolate.
8. With the mixer running at low speed, add about a quarter cup of melted butter and beat until fully absorbed.
9. Add one egg yolk and beat at low speed until incorporated.
10. Continue alternating additions of butter and egg yolk until all are used up, scraping the bowl with a rubber spatula and beating after each addition just until incorporated.
11. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
12. Place the pan on a cookie sheet (despite any leakage it may still happen). Put the whole thing in the oven.
13. Bake for 25 minutes. It will be soupy in the middle and look like a mistake, but unless you forgot to turn the heat on, it is done.
14. Allow the cake to cool on a rack, then refrigerate for a few hours until firm. It may be kept refrigerated for up to two days.
15. When ready to serve, cut around the side of the pan with a small, sharp knife and release and remove the side of the pan.
16. Cover the dessert with a flat plate and invert.
17. Carefully pry up and remove the bottom of the pan and the waxed paper.
18. Decorate with whipped cream. A simple lattice pattern works well so that the dark chocolate shows through.
19. Serves 12.
